title: convolvulus hepaticifolius
intro: Convolvulus hepaticifolius, also known as the liver bindweed, is a perennial herb native to Australia with hairy stems and leaves shaped like those of the liverwort. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and reducing pain. Consuming the plant in moderation may provide health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Convolvulus hepaticifolius can be used to make teas or infusions, as well as dried and added to capsules or tinctures for further use.
